Reading ASCII art with a model of the human brain

TRIBE v2 cross-modal probes — does a cat-art LoRA pull ASCII toward visual cortex? · GB10 / Blackwell · 2026-06-15

Two findings — one confirmed, one refuted. (1) Confirmed: TRIBE v2's video branch (V-JEPA 2) reads a real cat photo straight into visual cortex (5.48× enrichment), and the more faithful an img2ascii rendering is, the more cat-like its brain map becomes (full-res cos 0.55 > reduced-res 0.43). (2) Refuted at scale: a single hand-made iconic ASCII cat hinted that the cat-art finetune pulls ASCII-text toward visual cortex (0.85× → 2.05×) — but across 6 real cats this did not replicate: ASCII-as-text stays near-orthogonal to the image (cos ≈ 0.13) and the finetune's convergence gain is -0.006 (3/6 images). The hint was an artifact of feeding the LoRA exactly the iconic style it was trained on — the effect is stylistic, not a genuine text↔image cat-semantics bridge.

Setup: three encoders, two branches in play

TRIBE v2 predicts fMRI on the cortical surface (20484 vertices) from three frozen encoders — text = Llama-3.2-3B, audio = w2v-bert-2.0, video = V-JEPA 2. The ASCII-cat LoRA lives in the text branch, so it only moves text-routed inputs; images/bitmaps go through V-JEPA. We drive each branch in isolation (video-only vs word-only events) and compare maps in the same brain space.

Result 1 — the visual branch: a real cat lands in visual cortex; an ASCII cat half-does demonstrated

Real cat photo

ASCII-cat bitmap

stimulus (video branch)visuallanguageauditorycos to real cat
real cat photo5.48×0.29×0.00×— (anchor)
ASCII-cat bitmap3.64×2.76×0.58×0.435

The cos = 0.44 gap is the optimization headroom: "make ASCII whose V-JEPA map matches a real cat" is a well-posed objective.

Result 2 — the text branch: ASCII bypassing TTS, base vs cat-finetune demonstrated

We inject the ASCII glyphs straight into Llama (overwriting the event context), skipping the text→speech→transcribe path entirely.

base Llama → brain

cat finetune → brain

metric (ASCII as text)value
finetune effect — relative change of the brain map34%
vs. ordinary speech (phase-1 baseline)~8%
cos(base, finetune)0.940
visual enrichment: base → finetune0.85× → 2.05×

Feeding the model the kind of input its LoRA was trained on (a clean iconic ASCII cat), the finetune moves the representation ~4× harder than for speech and the shift is toward visual cortex. This was the exciting hint — but it is a single, in-distribution stimulus. Result 3 tests whether it survives real img2ascii at scale (it does not).

Result 3 — the scaled test: 6 real cats × a resolution ladder visual ladder ✓ convergence ✗

Each cat photo → real image (anchor) + full-res img2ascii + reduced-res img2ascii. We ask: (a) does a higher-res ASCII bitmap look more like the real cat to V-JEPA, and (b) does the finetune pull ASCII-text toward the real-image anchor?

example ladder (cat #0)

brain maps (cat #0)

metric (mean ± sd, n=6)full-resreduced-resreading
cos(anchor, ascii bitmap) 0.55 ± 0.19 0.43 ± 0.20 ↑ with resolution ✓
bitmap visual enrichment 3.98× 2.53× ↑ with resolution ✓
cos(anchor, ascii text, base) 0.13 0.08 ≈ orthogonal
finetune convergence gain -0.006 (3/6) -0.080 (1/6) null / negative ✗
ascii-text finetune rel.change 16% 23% real (> 8% speech)

The realism ladder holds; the convergence does not. More-faithful ASCII bitmaps do drive visual cortex more (full 0.55 > reduced 0.43). But ASCII-as-text never approaches the image anchor (cos ≈ 0.1) and the finetune does not close the gap — the Result-2 hint was specific to a clean iconic cat (in-distribution for the LoRA), and evaporates on realistic img2ascii.

The iconic ASCII cat (Result 2 stimulus)

  /\ ___ /\
 (  -   -  )
  \  ~ ~  /
  /       \
 /         \
 |         |
  \       /
  /// /// --

Synthesis & honesty

  • Solid: the visual realism ladder — img2ascii fidelity ↔ V-JEPA visual response — replicates across 6 cats. You can make ASCII a brain-model reads as more cat-like by improving the rendering.
  • Refuted: the cross-modal convergence hint (finetune makes ASCII-text more image-like). It held only for a single iconic ASCII cat — the exact style the LoRA was trained on — and did not survive realistic img2ascii (gain -0.006). The finetune's effect is stylistic/distributional, not cat-semantic — consistent with phase 1 (TRIBE is sensitive to the extractor's distribution, not its semantics).
  • Methodological takeaway: a single in-distribution probe can badly over-promise. The honest signal only appeared after scaling with out-of-distribution inputs.

Replicate

cd /home/ath/tribev2-research && source env.sh && cd work
python probe_visual.py        # video branch: real cat + ascii bitmap
python probe_text_ascii.py    # text branch: ascii-as-text, base vs finetune
python make_showcase.py       # rebuild this page
# scaled run:
python experiment_phase2.py   # N cats x resolution ladder, cross-modal convergence

TRIBE v2 · V-JEPA 2 video branch · Llama-3.2-3B text branch + ascii-cat LoRA · maps in work/demo_out/phase2/ · numbers recomputed from visual_maps.npz / text_ascii_maps.npz.
